description: Four balanced mapped fixtures for forward/reverse non-tetrahedral and assigned-atrop rule application. These fixtures validate supplied graph stereochemistry, not reaction selectivity, barriers, 3D inference, or physical configurational stability.
electronic_scope: Coordination cases use SynKit's declared scalar two-centre Lewis convention. Every endpoint passes the local formal-charge/resource audit. The octahedral cobalt center is explicitly Co+ because V=9, L=1, R=0, H=0, and six sigma bonds require q=+1.

provenance.representation_note: The ligand alphabets follow the non-tetrahedral representation families exercised by the RDKit Book and SynKit's pinned adapter fixtures; the transformations and expected outcomes are project-designed conformance cases.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit square-planar serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: A supplied geometric isomerization only; no pathway or energetic feasibility is asserted.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit TB serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: The endpoint inversion is supplied. No Berry-pseudorotation path, barrier, or preferred geometry is inferred.

cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit OH serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor application.
cases.claim_boundary: The endpoint inversion is supplied. No twist mechanism, barrier, or preferred geometry is inferred.

cases.event: ortho-substituted biaryl radical recombination with declared racemic axial outcome
cases.conversion_boundary: RDKit assigned-atrop serialization is checked after internal relative-descriptor branching.
cases.claim_boundary: The rule declares an assigned axial population. Connectivity alone is not used to infer rotational stability, barrier, or atropisomerism.

negative_controls.expected_detection: strict stereo guard rejects the inverse/non-equivalent input orientation
descriptor topology validation localizes the non-adjacent owner/reference errors
strict stereo guard rejects an otherwise isomorphic host with an empty registry
the two assigned-atrop product branches remain exact-stereo non-isomorphic and survive ITS deduplication
